rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA YEE LIN MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing in April 2023, and nothing has been filed since the previous two appearances: 1) A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 2) No bank statement verifying the ending cash balance of the Bank of America account is on file. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of the original statement must also be filed. LRSF 14.59(A). 3) The current bond is insufficient based on the personal property on hand and annual gross rental income. 4) Did the petitioner take any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a) during this account period? 5) The petition must be amended to request a continuance of administration for a specific period as additional relief sought, unless the estate is ready to petition for final distribution before this accounting can be settled. LRSF 14.35(J)(2). 6) An allegation must be made as to whether there are any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. LRSF 14.35(J)(1). 7) Is there any record showing time of death of the decedent and her spouse for the purpose of applying Probate Code section 223(b)? 8) Has the same tenant been occupying the estate real property? If so, please explain why rent amounts are different in some months. 9) Has the tenant paid a security deposit? No schedule of liabilities has been filed. 10) Are Anastazya, Annika, and Mattias the only natural descendants of the petitioner? Each minor's age must also be specified. 11) Is the petitioner the only person having legal custody of and residing with each minor descendant within the meaning of CRC 7.51(d)? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMMA CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/22/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 11/02/2023). The following issues remain unaddressed since the last hearing on 08/28/2023: 1) A proof must be filed to show that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by Probate Code sections 9730 and 10533(a)(2). 2) An original small estate affidavit signed by Denise Chew, together with a certified copy of Jane Leong's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 3) An original small estate affidavit signed by Robert Yee, Eric Yee, and Debra Yee, together with a certified copy of Cobina Wyatt's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 4) An original small estate affidavit signed by Valerie Smith, Sharon Chan, and Jeffrey Chan, together with a certified copy of Rose Chan's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 5) To the extent 1/3 of Cobina Wyatt's separate property interest in this estate passed to her spouse (Theron Wyatt) without administration under section 13500, an additional small estate affidavit signed by Robert Yee, Eric Yee, and Debra Yee, together with a certified copy of Theron Wyatt's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 6) To the extent 1/3 of Rose Chan's separate property interest in this estate passed to her spouse (Albert Chan) without administration under section 13500, an additional small estate affiavit signed by Valerie Smith, Sharon Chan, and Jeffrey Chan, together with a certified copy of Albert Chan's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed).
